description.en: Components of the Global Polypectomy Assessment Tool (GPAT).

label.en: Very poor
Very good
Tip control

guidance.en: Controlled, stable and purposeful

label.en: Fully appreciates/demonstrates extent of the polyp to be resected

guidance.en: Clearly appreciates entire extent of the polyp; approach and resection reflect this

label.en: Positioning with respect to the polyp

guidance.en: Lesion at or near 6 o'clock, close to the colonoscope, fluid lies away from lesion (good use of gravity)

label.en: Technique selected is appropriate for the polyp resected

guidance.en: Correct decision for en bloc vs. piecemeal resection, hot vs. cold appropriate for the polyp

label.en: Injection is performed in the correct plane

guidance.en: The submucosal plane is quickly found and injection rapidly results in sustained mucosal lifting (needle in submucosa)

label.en: Injection is performed dynamically

guidance.en: Once the needle is in the submucosa, there is graduated movement of the needle away from the muscularis towards the center of the lumen

label.en: Injection is used to improve lesion access

guidance.en: Injection clearly facilitates access to the target lesion

label.en: Appropriate snare size/type selected

guidance.en: Snare of appropriate size and type for the polyp

label.en: Stable position with lesion at 6 o'clock OR transformed to 6 o'clock

guidance.en: Snare position is consistently maintained at 6 o'clock and the position is stable

label.en: Maximizing snare capture

guidance.en: Good capture of polyp tissue within snare/use of downward pressure/use of gas aspiration resulting in complete capture of adequate target tissue

label.en: Snare precisely visualized during placement and closure (V of the snare)

guidance.en: Snare V visualized consistently during closure and near to the colonoscope

label.en: Residual tissue islands avoided if piecemeal resection or macroscopically complete if en bloc resection attempted

guidance.en: Snare placement includes > 2–3 mm normal margin (at edge) of tissue or uses transected tissue edge as a guide (within defect) resulting in no tissue islands/complete en bloc

label.en: Moves the closed snare to confirm independent movement from deeper structures

guidance.en: Checks mobility prior to transection with respect to deeper structures

label.en: Lifts the snare away from the muscularis propria prior to application of electrosurgical energy

guidance.en: Lifts the snare away from the muscularis prior to the application of electrosurgical energy

label.en: Mucosa: looks for, detects, and removes residual adenomatous tissue at margin and within defect

guidance.en: Ostensibly and systematically checks for residual adenomatous tissue within the defect and at the defect margin, and removes it successfully

label.en: Thermal ablation of the post-EMR margin

guidance.en: Steady systematic application, does not ablate visible polyp tissue, complete ablation of the entire margin achieved

label.en: Submucosa: looks for, detects, and treats any bleeding vessels within the defect

guidance.en: Detects and treats bleeding vessels in the submucosa; does not treat other submucosal appearances including herniating vessels

label.en: Muscularis: looks for, detects, and treats deep mural injury ≥II (Sydney classification)

guidance.en: Detects and treats types II–V deep mural injury or confirms they are not present

label.en: Placement of through-the-scope clips

guidance.en: Good use of suction, positioning, and rotation to capture required tissue and achieves secure appearing closure

label.en: Use of polyp retrieval device

guidance.en: 6 o'clock position, sequential place and retrieve technique applied, captures all pieces successfully

label.en: Use of coagulation grasper

guidance.en: Uses water to identify the causative vessel, confirms correct placement with cessation of bleeding after closure, tents vessel away from the muscularis to apply electrosurgical energy
